summary: The relay station system (ten sukkot from Jerusalem to the Azazel cliff), the designated man's method of pushing the goat backward off the cliff with the crimson thread tied between its horns, and the ruling that the Azazel service is valid even after nightfall.

display.whats: Ten relay stations were set up along the 90-ris route from Jerusalem to the Azazel cliff, each a mil apart (Shabbat boundary), except the last which was two mil away - so the final escort stopped short and watched from a distance.
display.hint: Why was the last station different from all the others?

learning.summary: The Mishnah states ten sukkot (relay stations) were built along the route from Jerusalem to the Azazel cliff. The Gemara works out the math: 90 ris equals 12 mil, divided into 11 stages. Each escort group accompanied the designated man exactly one mil (the Shabbat boundary), except the last group which was 2 mil from the cliff.
learning.ahaMoment: The whole relay system was designed around the Shabbat boundary: each escort could walk one mil out and return without violating Shabbat - except at the end where they permitted extra distance because the man was exhausted and afraid to be alone.
learning.memoryAnchor: Ten sukkot, each a mil apart - like pit stops on a desert marathon, with the last runner watching from afar.
learning.openQuestion: Why did the Sages permit the last escort to go beyond the Shabbat boundary?

argumentFlow.label: Ruling: Ten relay stations from Jerusalem to the cliff
argumentFlow.speaker: Mishnah
argumentFlow.text: Ten sukkot were built along the 90-ris route to the Azazel cliff, each with escorts.

argumentFlow.label: Reason: Distance calculation - 90 ris equals 12 mil
argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: Ninety ris at 7.5 ris per mil equals 12 mil, giving 11 stages - 10 between sukkot plus one from last sukkah to cliff.

argumentFlow.label: Digression: Discrepancy with R. Elazar Kalir's calculation
argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: Rashi notes the calculation differs from liturgical poet R. Elazar Kalir, who used a different standard for ris measurement.

argumentFlow.label: Ruling: Each escort walks one mil - Shabbat boundary
argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: Each mil is the Shabbat boundary - escorts could accompany the man exactly one mil and return.

argumentFlow.label: Ruling: Last escort stopped at one mil from cliff
argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: The last station was two mil from the cliff; the final escort accompanied him one mil and watched from a distance.

argumentFlow.label: Reason: Permitted extra distance because man was afflicted
argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: The Sages permitted the escort to go beyond the normal Shabbat limit because the man was exhausted and afraid to be alone in the wilderness after dark.

theological: communal escort as honor and support
narrative: crowds lining the way to the cliff

misconceptions.misconception: The designated man walked alone the entire 90-ris route
misconceptions.correction: He had relay escorts at each mile marker - only the final push to the cliff was alone.

visualizableElements.description: Ten sukkot spaced one mil apart along desert road from Jerusalem to cliff

quizSeeds.question: Why could each escort accompany the designated man exactly one mil?
quizSeeds.answer: One mil equals the Shabbat boundary - the escort could walk one mil out and still return within the permitted two-thousand-amot limit.

sugyot.finalRuling: Ten relay stations were arranged from Jerusalem to the cliff; each group escorted the designated man one mil, except the last escort who stopped a mile short of the cliff.

display.whats: The designated man pushed the Azazel goat backward off the cliff. Half the crimson thread was tied between the goat's horns; when the thread turned white, Israel's atonement was accepted.
display.hint: Why push the goat backward rather than simply letting it fall forward?

learning.summary: At the cliff, the designated man pushed the goat backward (al achorav) so it would somersault and not land on its feet. Half the crimson thread was tied between the goat's horns so the escort could watch for it turning white - the sign that Israel's sins were forgiven, based on Isaiah 1:18.
learning.ahaMoment: The thread turning white was not merely symbolic - it was real-time feedback that atonement had been accepted. The Talmud elsewhere records that in the last 40 years of the Second Temple the thread stopped turning white.
learning.memoryAnchor: The goat pushed backward so it could not land on its feet - and the red thread tied to its horns as atonement's status indicator.
learning.openQuestion: What theological message does the crimson-to-white transformation communicate about the nature of Divine forgiveness?

argumentFlow.label: Ruling: Goat pushed backward off cliff
argumentFlow.speaker: Mishnah
argumentFlow.text: The designated man pushed the goat backward (al achorav) off the cliff so it would tumble and die.

argumentFlow.label: Reason: Backward push prevents landing on feet
argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: He bent its head under its body so it fell on its back and could not see if the thread turned white before dying.

argumentFlow.label: Ruling: Half the crimson thread tied between goat's horns
argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: Half the crimson thread was tied between the goat's horns so the escort could watch for it turning white as a sign of atonement.

argumentFlow.label: Reason: Thread tied at the last moment to keep goat focused
argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: The thread was tied between the horns last so the goat's eyes would be fixed on it - ensuring the man would not stop pushing until the work was complete.

halachic: method of killing the Azazel goat
theological: Isaiah 1:18 - sins like crimson turning white as snow
narrative: the goat pushed backward off the cliff
thread between the horns as the last act

misconceptions.misconception: The goat simply fell off the cliff
misconceptions.correction: The designated man actively pushed it backward so it could not land on its feet and survive.

visualizableElements.description: Man at cliff top pushing goat backward; goat tumbling with crimson thread visible between horns

quizSeeds.question: Why was half the crimson thread tied between the goat's horns?
quizSeeds.answer: So the escort at the cliff could watch for it turning white - the sign that Israel's sins had been forgiven.

sugyot.finalRuling: The designated man pushed the Azazel goat backward off the cliff; half the crimson thread was tied between its horns as an atonement indicator.

display.whats: If the designated man and the Azazel goat did not reach the cliff before nightfall, the service is still valid - the Azazel dispatch is not subject to the daytime requirement that governs other Temple services.
display.hint: Does the Azazel service have a daytime requirement like other Temple offerings?

learning.summary: The Gemara addresses the case where the designated man and goat did not arrive at the Azazel cliff before Yom Kippur nightfall. The ruling is that the service remains valid - unlike most Temple services which are invalid after dark, the Azazel dispatch and the goat's death at the cliff are valid even at night.
learning.ahaMoment: The Azazel service is exceptional: most Temple work must be done by day, but since the goat's death at the cliff is not a Temple altar service in the classic sense, the nightfall cutoff does not apply.
learning.memoryAnchor: The goat can arrive after dark - the only Yom Kippur act that works at night.
learning.openQuestion: Why does the Azazel service uniquely escape the daytime requirement that governs all other Temple offerings?

argumentFlow.label: Question: What if goat did not reach cliff before nightfall?
argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: What is the law if the designated man and the goat did not arrive at the cliff before Yom Kippur nightfall?

argumentFlow.label: Ruling: Service valid even after nightfall
argumentFlow.speaker: Gemara
argumentFlow.text: The Azazel service is valid even if performed after nightfall - not subject to the daytime requirement of standard Temple services.

theological: Azazel service as expulsion rather than sacrifice
narrative: journey to distant cliff potentially extending past dark

requiresUnderstanding: general rule that Temple services are invalid at night

misconceptions.misconception: The Azazel service is invalid if performed at night like other Temple services
misconceptions.correction: The Azazel service at the cliff is valid even after nightfall - categorically different from altar offerings.

visualizableElements.description: Standard Temple service (must be day) vs Azazel cliff dispatch (valid at night)

quizSeeds.question: Is the Azazel service invalid if the goat does not reach the cliff before nightfall?
quizSeeds.answer: No - the Azazel service at the cliff is valid even after dark, unlike most Temple services.

sugyot.finalRuling: If the Azazel goat did not reach the cliff before nightfall, the service is still valid.

rashiTranslations.he: תשעים ריס. היו מירושלים ועד צוק הר גבוה וזקוף כדאמרי'
rashiTranslations.en: Ninety ris is the distance from Jerusalem to the cliff - tall and sheer, as stated in Bava Metzia.

rashiTranslations.he: בבבא מציעא עלתה לראשי צוקין ונפלה: שבעה ומחצה ריס לכל
rashiTranslations.en: The goat ascended to the cliff top and fell. Seven and a half ris per mil.

rashiTranslations.he: מיל. הרי לתשעים ריס י"ב מיל והם י"א מסעות מירושלים לסוכה
rashiTranslations.en: So ninety ris equals twelve mil, which is eleven stages from Jerusalem to the first sukkah.

rashiTranslations.he: הראשונה מסע אחד ומסוכה לסוכה עד י' סוכות ט' מסעות הרי
rashiTranslations.en: One stage from Jerusalem to the first sukkah; from sukkah to sukkah through ten sukkot is nine stages.

rashiTranslations.he: י' מסעות ומסוכה אחרונה לצוק מסע
rashiTranslations.en: Ten stages, and from the last sukkah to the cliff is one stage.

rashiTranslations.he: אחד הרי לכל מסע ומסע מיל חוץ
rashiTranslations.en: Thus each stage is one mil except one which is two mil.

rashiTranslations.he: מאחד מהן שיש בו ב' מילין היינו
rashiTranslations.en: That is the one that is two mil - this explains why the escorts go from sukkah to sukkah.

rashiTranslations.he: דקתני ומלוין אותו מסוכה לסוכה
rashiTranslations.en: Because one mil is the Shabbat boundary, except for the last one.

rashiTranslations.he: שהמיל הוא תחום שבת חוץ מאחרון
rashiTranslations.en: Which is two mil away; the escorts accompany him one mil.

rashiTranslations.he: שהוא רחוק ב' מילין ומלויהו מיל
rashiTranslations.en: And stand at a distance watching what he does.

rashiTranslations.he: ועומד מרחוק ורואה את מעשיו
rashiTranslations.en: This calculation does not match the calculation of Rabbi Elazar Kalir.

rashiTranslations.he: והחשבון הזה אינו שוה לחשבון שיסד
rashiTranslations.en: And in thirty reeds he measured the ris - that calculation does not equal two thousand amot per mil.

rashiTranslations.he: רבי אלעזר קליר ובשלשים קנים
rashiTranslations.en: But in the Midrash to Haazinu (Sifri) we find the measurement for dividing the Land of Israel.

rashiTranslations.he: הוא קצב הריס אותו חשבון אינו עולה
rashiTranslations.en: The width of each tribe's boundary is seventy-five mil.

rashiTranslations.he: לאלפים אמה המיל אבל בברייתא
rashiTranslations.en: Also Rabbi Elazar Kalir composed it thus in his piyut Erchatz b'Nikon Kapot.

rashiTranslations.he: דהאזינו בספרי מצינו שם מידת חלוקת
rashiTranslations.en: His borders - twenty-five thousand reeds as in the book of Ezekiel.

rashiTranslations.he: ארץ ישראל לעתיד לבוא רוחב גבול
rashiTranslations.en: As it says 'from this measure you shall measure' - each reed is six amot.

rashiTranslations.he: כל שבט ושבט שבעים וחמשה מיל
rashiTranslations.en: That is one hundred fifty thousand amot; divide by two thousand amot per mil.

rashiTranslations.he: וגם רבי אלעזר קליר יסד כן בפיוט
rashiTranslations.en: That equals seventy-five mil.

rashiTranslations.he: ארחץ בנקיון כפות ברוחב ע"ה מיל
rashiTranslations.en: Aretz b'Nikon with a width of seventy-five mil.

rashiTranslations.he: חריצותיה והם כ"ה אלף קנים שבספר
rashiTranslations.en: Its borders are twenty-five thousand reeds from the book of Ezekiel.

rashiTranslations.he: יחזקאל (מה) שנאמר ומן המדה
rashiTranslations.en: As stated: 'from this measure you shall measure' etc.

rashiTranslations.he: הזאת תמוד וגו' וכל קנה ו' אמות
rashiTranslations.en: And each reed is six amot.

rashiTranslations.he: הרי ק"נ אלף אמה תן לכל אלפים
rashiTranslations.en: That is one hundred fifty thousand amot - seventy-five mil.

rashiTranslations.he: אמה מיל הרי ע"ה מיל: הרי מים
rashiTranslations.en: Amah per mil - seventy-five mil. Food and water.

rashiTranslations.he: והרי מזון. בגמרא מפרש אינו דומה
rashiTranslations.en: Food and water. The Gemara explains: one who has bread in his basket is not like one without.

rashiTranslations.he: מי שיש לו פת בסלו למי שאין לו:
rashiTranslations.en: One who has bread in his basket compared to one without.

rashiTranslations.he: שאינו מגיע עמו לצוק. כמו שפירשתי
rashiTranslations.en: He does not reach the cliff with him - as I explained.

rashiTranslations.he: לפי שהוא רחוק ב' מילין: חולק לשון
rashiTranslations.en: Because it is two mil away. He divides the crimson thread.

rashiTranslations.he: של זהורית. בגמרא מפרש טעמא:
rashiTranslations.en: Of the crimson wool. The Gemara explains the reason.

rashiTranslations.he: בא וישב לו. חוזר עד סוכה האחרונה
rashiTranslations.en: He comes and sits at the last sukkah - returns from the cliff.

rashiTranslations.he: אע"פ שהיוצא חוץ לתחום אפילו
rashiTranslations.en: Even though one who leaves the Shabbat boundary, even with permission.

rashiTranslations.he: ברשות אין לו אלא אלפים אמה
rashiTranslations.en: Has only two thousand amot from where he left with permission.

rashiTranslations.he: ממקום שיצא לשם ברשות לזה התירו
rashiTranslations.en: For this man they permitted it.

rashiTranslations.he: לפי שהוא מעונה וחלש ועוד שירא
rashiTranslations.en: Because he is afflicted and weak, and also afraid.

rashiTranslations.he: לעמוד יחידי במדבר משתחשך:
rashiTranslations.en: To stand alone in the wilderness after dark.

rashiTranslations.he: מאימתי מטמא בגדים. דכתיב והמשלח
rashiTranslations.en: From when does he become tamei in his garments? As written: 'the one who sends the goat shall wash his garments.'

rashiTranslations.he: את השעיר יכבס בגדיו (ויקרא טז) :
rashiTranslations.en: The one who sends the goat shall wash his garments (Leviticus 16).

rashiTranslations.he: גמ' חמש סוכות ועשרה מילין.
rashiTranslations.en: Gemara: five sukkot and ten mil.

rashiTranslations.he: וכל אחד מלויהו יותר ממיל או עד
rashiTranslations.en: Each one escorts him more than a mil or until the sukkah through eruv.

rashiTranslations.he: הסוכה על יד עירוב: סח לי אלעזר
rashiTranslations.en: My son Elazar told me - in a tone of amusement about my words.

rashiTranslations.he: בני. בלשון שחוק על דבריי ואמר
rashiTranslations.en: He said: if through eruv, two sukkot would suffice.

rashiTranslations.he: לי אם ע"י עירוב דיי לנו בב' סוכות
rashiTranslations.en: The people of Jerusalem escort him two mil and from the first sukkah they go out to meet him two mil.

rashiTranslations.he: שיהיו בני ירושלים מלוין אותו שני
rashiTranslations.en: That is four mil you can distance the sukkah from Jerusalem.

rashiTranslations.he: מילין ומסוכה ראשונה יצאו לקראתו
rashiTranslations.en: And the second sukkah at the end of the first four mil.

rashiTranslations.he: ב' מילין הרי ד' מילין שאתה יכול
rashiTranslations.en: Some of the first sukkah people make an eruv toward the second.

rashiTranslations.he: להרחיק הסוכה מירושלים וסוכה
rashiTranslations.en: And some make an eruv toward Jerusalem.

rashiTranslations.he: השניה לסוף ארבעה מילין של
rashiTranslations.en: And the second sukkah people - some make an eruv toward the first.

rashiTranslations.he: ראשונה ויהיו מקצת בני הראשונה
rashiTranslations.en: To go out to meet him two mil.

rashiTranslations.he: מערבין לצד השניה ומקצת יערבו
rashiTranslations.en: And some make an eruv toward the cliff to escort him two mil.

rashiTranslations.he: לצד ירושלים וכן בני שניה
rashiTranslations.en: According to whom? According to Rabbi Meir.

rashiTranslations.he: מקצתן מערבין לצד הראשונה
rashiTranslations.en: For according to Rabbi Yehudah and Rabbi Yosi, even the last one reaches the cliff.

rashiTranslations.he: לצאת לקראתו ב' מילין ומקצתן
rashiTranslations.en: A person does not need this - the sender does not need to eat on the way.

rashiTranslations.he: מערבין לצד צוק ללוותו ב' מילין:
rashiTranslations.en: What is the reason they told him this? Because one who has bread in his basket is not like one who is hungry.

rashiTranslations.he: כמאן כרבי מאיר. דאילו לרבי
rashiTranslations.en: Since there is a mitzva with the goat - to push it backward, as it is written 'to an arid land'.

rashiTranslations.he: יהודה ורבי יוסי אף האחרון מגיע לצוק: לא צריך אדם לכך.
rashiTranslations.en: Something cut off and descending. If they held it completely on the rock, sometimes.

rashiTranslations.he: לא נצרך אדם המשלח לאכול בדרך אלא מה טעם אמרו לו כן
rashiTranslations.en: The thread turns white before pushing the goat and they are satisfied by the whitening of sins.

rashiTranslations.he: שאינו דומה מי שיש לו פת בסלו להיות רעב כמי שאין לו:
rashiTranslations.en: And do not care to fulfill the mitzva of the goat. Therefore he ties the second half between its horns.

rashiTranslations.he: כיון דמצוה בשעיר. לדחפו לאחוריו כדכתיב אל ארץ גזירה
rashiTranslations.en: Its eyes fixed on it; and since he is occupied with it he will not refrain from pushing it.

rashiTranslations.he: דבר המתגזר ויורד אי אמרת נקטריה כוליה בסלע זמנין
rashiTranslations.en: Until the work is complete. He bends its head under its body when falling backward.

rashiTranslations.he: דקדים ומלבין קודם דחיפת השעיר ומייתבא דעתיה משמחת
rashiTranslations.en: So it cannot see whether the thread has turned white.

rashiTranslations.he: ליבון החטאים ולא איכפת ליה לקיים מצות השעיר לפיכך קושר
rashiTranslations.en: Whitening of sins satisfies them and they do not care to complete the mitzva of the goat. Therefore he ties the second half between its horns.

rashiTranslations.he: באחרונה חציה בין קרניו ועיניו תלוין בו ומאחר שהוא עסוק בו באחרונה לא יניח מלדחפו והיא לא תלבין לחצאים עד
rashiTranslations.en: Between its horns, its eyes fixed on it - and since he is occupied with it at the last moment, he will not refrain from pushing it.

rashiTranslations.he: שתגמר מלאכת כולה: דגמיש ליה לרישיה. יכוף ראשו תחת גופו כשיפול לאחוריו ולא יוכל לראות הלשון אם הלבין:
rashiTranslations.en: Until the entire task is complete. He bends its head - bends its head under its body when falling backward so it cannot right itself.

rashiTranslations.he: דכתיב
rashiTranslations.en: As it is written.
